channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI for lawyers: 10 tips to help integrate new tools into legal work
item.description: Lawyers tend to be the butt of jokes about not being human, so perhaps it’s no surprise that AI has been widely embraced in the legal world.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: How LinkedIn opened the door to AI slop
item.description: LinkedIn is covered in unreadable corporate garble. AI could be to blame. A new study by AI detection startup Originality AI reveals just how thoroughly AI-assisted content has invaded LinkedIn’s feed.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Robotic shorts give your legs a boost to walk farther and tire less
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how WalkON enhances walking and jogging with sensors and artificial tendons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AWS Ups Database Migration Service with AI-Powered Schema Conversion
item.description: Amazon Web Services updates its Database Migration Service's schema conversion feature with a new generative AI-powered capability
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What’s holding back CIO’s AI strategies? Their own AI learning curve for one
item.description: Under pressure to deploy AI within their organizations, most CIOs fear they don’t have the knowledge they need about the fast-changing technology.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why Sovereign Cloud is essential for today’s businesses
item.description: So, why suddenly does everyone care about “where the data lives?” Well, the truth is, we’ve always cared. Policymakers interested in leveraging data sovereignty requirements to advance economic and investment goals certainly continue to care.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How AI is accelerating—and devaluing—book publishing
item.description: AI has the power to pump out words in record speed. And already, that’s substantially inflating the book publishing market. Lovers of LLMs are pushing into the traditional publishing system, building their own book-publishing entities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday. Two senior executives, David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus, will take over as interim co-CEOs amid the search for a new chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Is your friend's phone number compromised? Here's what to look for
item.description: That weird text from a friend may actually be from a hacker. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson tells you what signs to look out for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2025 will be a bad year for remote work
item.description: “Mr. Musk Goes to Washington.” And he’s planning to roll back remote work in the federal government. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk and one-time presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy are expected to be appointed by incoming president Donald J.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: As temperatures turn frigid, Minnesotans turn to saunas for warmth and community
item.description: As temperatures drop into the teens, Minnesotans have embraced sauna culture for warmth and community.
item.dc:creator: Michael Goldberg And Mark Vancleave, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 9 ways scammers can use your phone number to try to trick you
item.description: Scammers can wreak havoc on your life just by having your phone number. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson addresses how to avoid becoming a victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: Thousands of Amazon workers around the world went on strike Friday, demanding more pay and better working conditions during one of the retail giant's busiest weekends of the year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FTC opens antitrust investigation into Microsoft’s cloud, AI, and cybersecurity practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, scrutinizing its cloud computing, software licensing, cybersecurity, and AI businesses. The inquiry follows over a year of informal interviews with competitors and business partners, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel’s $7.9B subsidy deal comes at a high price for the chipmaker
item.description: Intel’s $7.86 billion subsidy agreement with the US government imposes strict conditions on the company’s ability to divest stakes in its chipmaking division if the unit becomes independent.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use ref structs in C# 13
item.description: In the C# programming language, structs or structure types are value types that allow faster access because they are usually stored in the stack.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kai Trump shows Elon Musk awkwardly tutoring Trump in rocket science
item.description: While Trump's aspiring influencer granddaughter happily shared video of her joining him at Musk's latest SpaceX launch, she also revealed moments of the president-elect looking 'out of depth' and 'intimidated.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10+ tips and services for new Bluesky users
item.description: For the past two or three weeks, upstart social network Bluesky has been attracting large numbers of new users every day, a crowd that quite evidently includes many Apple device users — the app has at times topped Apple’s App Store charts in the run-up to Thanksgiving in the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gov. Newsom sets himself up as a foil to Elon Musk and Donald Trump
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- As Elon Musk prepares to wield more power in Washington, the state where he built his fortune is girding for a fight.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
